rationale: The citing paper is an invited methodological synthesis/review (including a 1998–2003 JMR article audit), while the cited paper is a 2000 JMR empirical scanner-model study with specific dynamic lead/lag and diagnostic choices. Because the cited work could plausibly be used as a concrete methodological example in the review, but the provided evidence has no in-text citation context beyond a DOI match, it is not safe to drop as obvious mention-only.
